gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/462)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/462)
text: مَرَّ بِي أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ أَنَا أَمْشِي عَرْضَ نَاقَتِي فَقَالَ مَا لَكَ لَا تَرْكَبُ فَقُلْتُ ضَعُفَتْ نَاقَتِي فَأَرَدْتُ أَنْ أُخَفِّفَ عَنْهَا فَقَالَ رَحِمَكَ اللَّهُ ارْكَبْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يَحْمِلُ عَنِ الضَّعِيفِ وَ الْقَوِيِّ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from his father, from Muhamad Bin Amro, from Suleyman Al Rahhal, from Abdullah Bin Abu Yafour who said,

‘I passed by Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> and I was walking in the presence of my she-camel, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘What is the matter you are not riding? ‘ So I said, ‘My camel has weakened, so I wanted to lighten from it’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘May Allah<sup>azwj</sup> have Mercy on you! Ride, for Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Carries from the weak and the strong’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from his father from Muhammad ibn ‘Amr from Sulayman al-Rahhal from ibn abu Ya‘fur who has said the following: “Once, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, passed by me and I was walking next to my camel. He (the Imam) asked, ‘Why are you not riding?’ I replied, ‘My camel has become weak, thus I decided to provide it relief.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘May Allah grant you blessings. Ride your camel, Allah carries for both the weak and the strong.’”
